summary:
# Summary

The provided text is a static informational resource document titled "Stardew Valley Resources," attributed to Lag and dated 2024. It does not constitute an empirical study, theoretical article, or narrative essay. Instead, it functions as a curated bibliography and policy statement associated with GameRT, an initiative likely linked to the American Library Association (ALA) given the email domain provided. The document is structured into four primary sections: social media links, guides, play-alike recommendations, and organizational policies/contact information.

The first section directs users to the official Twitter account for *Stardew Valley* and its developer, ConcernedApe (@ConcernedApe). This establishes a connection to the primary creators of the game, ensuring that users can access authentic updates and announcements. The second section provides a single link to the *Stardew Valley Wiki*, a community-maintained database of in-game information, serving as a reference tool for players seeking detailed mechanics or lore.

The third and most extensive section lists "Play Alikes," categorized by medium. This curation suggests an intent to help librarians or educators identify similar titles for programming or collection development. The board game category includes the official *Stardew Valley Board Game* as well as distinct farming and resource management titles such as *Viticulture*, *Agricola*, and *Fields of Arle*. The video game list features titles known for similar "cozy" or simulation aesthetics, including *Harvest Moon*, *Strange Horticulture*, *Graveyard Keeper*, *My Time at Portia*, *Slime Rancher*, *Forager*, and *Animal Crossing*. Notably, the text also includes Tabletop Role-Playing Games (TTRPGs) such as *Take Root*, *The Farmstead*, *Wishing Well*, and *Cozy Town*, indicating a broad interpretation of "games" that extends beyond digital formats to include narrative-driven tabletop experiences.

The fourth section outlines a five-point Twitch policy, presumably for live-streamed gaming events hosted by GameRT. The policy emphasizes community safety and respect, explicitly prohibiting hate speech and reserving the right to remove disruptive users. It provides clear protocols for reporting harassment to moderators and encourages private communication for sensitive issues. This reflects best practices for managing online public spaces in library or educational contexts.

Finally, the document provides contact information for GameRT, including links to a blog, Facebook pages, public resources, and a general website, along with an email address (gamert@ala.org). The presence of the ALA domain strongly suggests that this resource is intended for library professionals seeking to integrate games like *Stardew Valley* into their services.

In the context of Games-Based Library Services (GBLS), this text serves as a practical toolkit rather than a scholarly analysis. It highlights the interconnectedness of different game formats and underscores the importance of community guidelines in digital gaming environments. While it lacks empirical data or theoretical depth, it provides actionable references for librarians looking to expand their gaming collections or host related events. The inclusion of TTRPGs alongside video games is particularly relevant for GBLS literature, as it demonstrates a holistic approach to game-based engagement that transcends platform boundaries. However, due to its nature as a resource list, no findings regarding user behavior, learning outcomes, or service efficacy can be derived from this text.
sourceText:
Games Stardew Valley
Social Media Stardew Valley, Concernedape Twitter @ConcernedApe
Guides Stardew Valley
● https://stardewvalleywiki.com/Stardew_Valley_Wiki
Play Alikes
Board Games ● Stardew Valley Board Game ● Viticulture by Stonemaier Games ● Agricola by Mayfair Games ● Fields of Arle by Z-Man Games
Video Games ● Harvest Moon ● Strange Horticulture ● Graveyard Keeper ● My Time at Portia ● Slime Rancher ● Forager ● Animal Crossing
TTRPG ● Take Root by katykoop ● The Farmstead by Mikey Hamm ● Wishing Well by Riley Hopkins ● Cozy Town by Rae Nedjadi
Twitch Policy
1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT

Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
